items.label: storefront-dev

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ # Wait for server to be ready ↵ for i in $(seq 1 60); do ↵ if curl -s -o /dev/null -w

messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure:
The override files already exist. Let me check their current state:
Good - the implementation files already exist and look well-written. Let me run the tests to verify they pass:
All 30 tests pass with 100% code coverage. Now let me validate the dev server with SSR:
All routes return HTTP 200. The SSR is working correctly. Now let me check if there's a validation hook file to update:

The validation hook already has the baseline route checks including PLP routes. Since I didn't create any new routes (just component overrides for existing pages), the existing checks are sufficient. Let me check the git status:
The implementation files are already committed — only pipeline state files show as modified. Let me verify the files are tracked:
The implementation was already committed in earlier commits. The tests were also updated and committed. Let me verify there are no uncommitted changes in the storefront files:

No uncommitted changes in the overrides directory. All implementation files are committed. The implementation is complete:  1. ✅ **ProductTile override** (`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx`) — wraps base tile with Quick View overlay bar 2. ✅ **QuickViewModal** (`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx`) — Chakra modal with ProductView + useProductViewModal 3. ✅ **ProductTile tests** (`overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js`) — 14 tests covering overlay bar rendering, interaction, accessibility 4. ✅ **QuickViewModal tests** (`overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js`) — 16 tests covering modal shell, content, error state, accessibility 5. ✅ **All 30 tests pass** with 100% code coverage 6. ✅ **SSR validation** — all routes (/, /category/newarrivals, /search?q=shirt) return HTTP 200 7. ✅ **Validation hooks** already have PLP/search route checks  Since everything is already committed and verified, I'll report completion:

reportedOutcome.docNote: Product Quick View feature implemented via PWA Kit override pattern: ProductTile override adds a full-width semi-transparent overlay bar (slide-up on desktop hover, always visible on mobile) that opens a QuickViewModal using the base template's useProductViewModal hook and ProductView component. Sets/bundles excluded. 30 unit tests pass at 100% coverage. SSR validated on all affected routes (/, /category/newarrivals, /search?q=shirt).
